projects.description: Analyzed the company's posture scope, goals, and assessment report, and conducted an internal audit by completing the controls and compliance checklist.
projects.title: Scope, Goals, and Risk Assessment
projects.overview: Botium Toys initiated an internal IT audit after detecting security and compliance risks affecting key assets.

sections.heading: Scenario
sections.content: Botium Toys, a growing U.S. toy company with an expanding online market, initiated an internal IT audit to strengthen its security posture and ensure compliance with industry and international regulations. Using the NIST Cybersecurity Framework, the IT manager outlined the audit scope, goals, and risk assessment. The task is to review this information and complete a controls & compliance checklist to identify security gaps, risks, and areas for improvement.

sections.heading: Scope
sections.content: The scope of this audit is defined as the entire security program at Botium Toys. This includes their assets like employee equipment and devices, their internal network, and their systems. The requirement is to review Botium Toys' assets and evaulate existing controls and compliance practices.

sections.heading: Goals
sections.content: Assess existing assets and complete the controls and compliance checklist to determine which controls and compliance best practices that need to be implemented to improve Botium Toys’ security posture.

items.content: Currently, there is inadequate management of assets. Additionally, Botium Toys does not have all of the proper controls in place and may not be fully compliant with U.S. and international regulations and standards.

The first of the five functions of the NIST CSF is Identify. Botium Toys will need to dedicate resources to identify assets so they can appropriately manage them. Additionally, they will need to classify existing assets and determine the impact of the loss of existing assets, including systems, on business continuity.
On a scale of 1 to 10, the risk score is 8, which is fairly high. This is due to a lack of controls and adherence to compliance best practices.

sections.heading: Recommendations
items: Implement Principle of Least Privilege Access
Set-up password management system
Assess current assets and legacy systems, and identify possible risks
Create an action plan to apply preventative / corrective measures to all assets, from highest to lowest risk priority
Identify all control categories (Administrative/Managerial, Technical, Physical/Operational) and their associated risks. Then, include them in the action plan
Collaborate with internal teams / employees to determine any additional risks on existing assets

projects.description: Examined DNS and ICMP traffic in transit using data from a network protocol analyzer tool and determined which network protocol was affected during this incident.
projects.title: Cybersecurity Incident Report: Network Traffic Analysis
projects.overview: DNS and ICMP packet analysis using tcpdump to determine the root cause of a network connectivity issue.

sections.heading: Scenario
sections.content: You are a cybersecurity analyst working at a company that specializes in providing IT services for clients. Several customers of clients reported that they were not able to access the client company website www.yummyrecipesforme.com, and saw the error “destination port unreachable” after waiting for the page to load. The task involves analyzing the situation to determine which network protocol was affected during this incident.

sections.heading: Problem Summary
sections.content: Accessing the company website returned a 'destination port unreachable' error. The UDP message requesting for an IP address with the domain name 'www.yummyrecipesforme.com' did not reach the DNS server on port 53, preventing the destination IP from receiving the request.

sections.heading: Findings
items: Several customers reported their inability to access the client customer website and got error “destination port unreachable” after waiting for the page to load
Based on the tcpdump logs, the error started at 1:24pm (similar errors found at 1:27pm and 1:28pm)

When the source IP 192.51.100.15 (browser) sent a connection to the destination IP 203.0.113.2 (DNS server), it generated a query identification number: 35084, and an 'A?' flag indicating an A record associated with the domain name mapping to an IP address
Tcpdump logs show an ICMP error response 'udp port 53 unreachable'
sections.content: This issue occurred because that there was no service listening on the receiving DNS port, blocking the domain name resolution and preventing subsequent HTTPS requests from reaching the web server.

sections.heading: Analysis
content: The incident started at 1:24pm and similar error messages were logged at 1:27pm and 1:28pm.

The IT team became aware of the incident as several customers reported that they were unable to access the client company website 'www.yummyrecipesforme.com' and saw error 'destination port unreachable' after waiting for the page to load.

To investigate the incident, the IT department attempted to visit the client company website and received the same error message as reported. The team also tried to troubleshoot the issue using the network analyzer tool, tcpdump, and attempted to load the website again. Lastly, they used the logs from the network analyzer tool to analyze the error messages.

skills: Identification of Network Attacks (DoS, Network Intrusion, IP Spoofing)
projects.description: Assessed Wireshark TCP/HTTP logs to identify the type of attack causing network interruption and evaluate its impact on web server performance.
projects.title: Cybersecurity Incident Report: Network Attack Assessment
projects.overview: TCP/HTTP log analysis using Wireshark to identify the cause of a network interruption.

sections.heading: Scenario
content: You work as a security analyst for a travel agency that advertises sales and promotions on the company’s website. An automated alert indicated a problem with the company's web server, followed by connection timeout errors. Packet capture revealed an abnormally high volume of TCP SYN requests from an unfamiliar IP address that overwhelmed the server's capacity to respond to legitimate traffic.

The server was taken offline to recover, and the source IP was temporarily blocked at the firewall. The incident required escalation due to the likelihood of a SYN flood attack and the need for longer-term mitigation to prevent recurrence.

sections.heading: Findings
items: An automated alert was received from the monitoring system indicating a problem with the company's web server
Upon visiting the company's website, a connection timeout error message is displayed on the browser
Packet capture using Wireshark revealed that usually high volume of TCP SYN packets coming from source IP 203.0.113.0
Initial requests from the malicious source IP 203.0.113.0 completed the 3-way TCP handshake, but subsequent SYN requests exhausted the company web server's capacity to respond to legitimate traffic
Company users (source IP 194.51.100.0/24) were able to access the website prior to the attack, but began experiencing connection timeouts when the malicious activity started
Logs showed [RST, ACK] packets and HTTP Gateway Timout responses resulting from unacknowledged SYN requests

content: The logs show a connection timeout response from the company’s website to the user’s browser when trying to access the website, indicating a SYN flood attack. If not addressed promptly, this event could be posing a much bigger risk.

Log detection, log analysis, and containment have been performed to temporarily prevent further damage on the company’s assets by taking the server offline to allow recovery and restore normal operations, as well as configuring the company’s firewall to block the IP address that sends the abnormal volume of SYN requests.

items.content: Normally, a TCP three-way handshake occurs with SYN → SYN-ACK → ACK packets to establish a connection. During the incident, a SYN flood attack sent an abnormally high volume of SYN packets to the company’s web server, exhausting its resources and preventing legitimate TCP connections. Packet logs show that the malicious IP repeatedly sent SYN packets, resulting in RST-ACK and Gateway Timeout responses and disrupting normal web service operations.

projects.description: Investigated a security event by reviewing tcpdump logs to identify the network protocols used and documenting what occurred during the incident.
projects.title: Security Incident Report
projects.overview: Tcpdump log analysis to investigate, identify, document, and recommend a solution for a security issue affecting access to the company's main webpage.

sections.heading: Scenario
content: You are a cybersecurity analyst for yummyrecipesforme.com, a website that sells recipes and cookbooks. A former employee has decided to lure users to a fake website with malware.

The objective of this investigation is to document the incident in detail, identify the network protocols used to establish the connection between the user and the website, and recommend a security action to take to prevent brute force attacks in the future.

sections.heading: Findings
items: Multiple customers reported to yummyrecipesforme's helpdesk that the company website prompted a file download, redirected to a different URL, and caused their computers to slow down
The website owner was unable to access the admin panel and contacted the website hosting provider for assistance
The security team created a sandbox environment and ran the network protocol analyzer (tcpdump) to observe suspicious website behavior
The web server was impacted by a brute force attack, as the disgruntled employee easily guessed the administrative account's default password
No preventive controls were in place to prevent such attacks

items.content: The network protocol involved in the brute force attack was HTTP. Since the website was using an unsecured protocol, it was easier for the malicious actor to perform an attack by accessing the admin panel and changing the source code.

content: Several customers reported that the company’s website prompted a file download, redirected them to 'greatrecipesforme.com', and caused their computers to slow down. Investigation identified a brute force attack that allowed the attacker to access the website and modify its source code.
The security team reproduced the issue in a sandbox environment using a network protocol analyzer, confirming the download prompt and subsequent redirection.

Tcpdump logs showed that the browser resolved 'yummyrecipesforme.com' via DNS, requested the webpage, initiated the malware download, and then resolved and accessed the malicious website ('greatrecipesforme.com'), completing the HTTP request.

Further analysis revealed that a malicious JavaScript code was embedded in the website's source code, prompting users to download an executable file. The use of HTTP left the connection unsecured, increasing vulnerability to attacks, and no controls were in place to prevent brute force attempts.

sections.heading: Recommendations
items: Implement HTTPS to secure connections using TLS/SSL certificates
Enforce strong password policies, including complex password requirements and periodic changes
Disallow reuse of previous passwords to enhace account security
Require 2-factor authentication (2FA) or multi-factor authentication (MFA) on company websites
Monitor login attempts and limit the number of failed attempts to prevent brute force attacks

skills: Understanding of Network Hardening Tools and Practices
projects.description: Performed a security risk assessment to evaluate a data breach and proposed network hardening controls to improve overall security.
projects.title: Security Risk Assessment
projects.overview: A social media organization initiated a network hardening review following a data breach incident.

sections.heading: Scenario
content: A social media organization experienced a major data breach that exposed customer personal information. In response, the organization wants to implement strong network hardening practices that can be performed consistently to prevent attacks and breaches in the future.

A network assessment identified four major vulnerabilities, including shared employee passwords, default administrative credentials, lack of firewall traffic filtering rules, and the absence of multi-factor authentication (MFA). Without remediation, these vulnerabilities significantly increase the likelihood of recurring breaches and other security incidents.

items.content: The organization's employees share passwords, increasing the risk of unauthorized access.
The admin password for the database is set to the default, making the database highly vulnerable to brute force attacks and potential data leaks.
The firewalls do not have rules in place to filter inbound and outbound network traffic, exposing the network to malicious activity.
Multi-factor authentication (MFA) is not used, increasing the risk of unauthorized access to sensitive data.

items.subheading: Enforce strong password policies with regular updates
items.content: This ensures that each user has a complex, unique password, distinct from the administrative account, that is changed regularly.

items.subheading: Use port filtering with firewall maintenance
items.content: Port filtering restricts company network access only to authorized IP addresses and secure websites, while firewall maintenance ensures that firewall rules remain current and effective.
This requires both user password and a one-time passcode (OTP) to grant access to authorized users only.

projects.projectName: Security Incident Reporting Using the NIST Cybersecurity Framework (CSF)
skills: Understanding and Applying the NIST CSF
projects.description: Created a security incident report aligned with the NIST Cybersecurity Framework (CSF) to analyze a network incident.
projects.title: Security Incident Reporting Using the NIST Cybersecurity Framework (CSF)
projects.overview: Network incident analysis and reporting using the NIST Cybersecurity Framework (CSF).

sections.heading: Scenario
content: A multimedia company recently experienced a distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attack that disrupted internal network operations for two hours.

The attack involved a flood of ICMP packets that overwhelmed the network services and prevented access to internal resources. The incident management team responded by blocking incoming ICMP packets, stopping all non-critical network services offline, and restoring critical network services.
Investigation later confirmed that the attack exploited an unconfigured firewall, allowing the malicious attacker to penetrate the company network.

items.content: The investigation revealed that an unconfigured firewall was used by the malicious actor to exploit the company network with DDoS attacks, specifically the Ping of Death. Additionally, the absence of intrusion detection and prevention systems prevented timely detection and alerting of the attack.

The security team strengthened the company's internal networks by implementing a new firewall to limit the rate of incoming ICMP packets, a source IP verification to check for spoofed IP addresses on incoming ICMP packets, a network monitoring software to detect abnormal traffic patterns, and an IDS/IPS system to filter ICMP traffic.

content: The security team implemented an IDS/IPS system to filter incoming ICMP traffic based on suspicious characteristics / patterns and for better alerting when malicious attacks occur.
Additionally, the organization can leverage SIEM dashboards and tools, tcpdump logs, and network log analyzer to monitor malicious activities.

System logging and monitoring, combined with security hardening procedures such as regular software updates / patches, disabling unused ports, reducing access permissions, and using MFA, can help identify abnormal network behavior and ensure that only authorized users have access to the internal network.
To address the incident and avoid further impact, the security team blocked the incoming ICMP packets, took all non-critical network services offline, and restored critical network services.

The team also deployed a new firewall to limit the rate of incoming ICMP packets, enabled a source IP verification to check for spoofed IP addresses, implemented a network monitoring software to detect abnormal traffic patterns, and activated an IDS/IPS system to filter suspicious ICMP activity.

The security team successfully restored normal network operations within 2 hours by performing thorough investigation and implementing the necessary controls to stop the DDoS attacks from compromising the internal network.

To improve response and recovery for future incidents, the organization implemented an IDS/IPS system to enhance detection, monitoring, and response to similar attacks. In addition, the use of SIEM tools, tcpdump logs, and network log analyzer supports faster and more effective incident resolution.

projects.projectName: Quantitative Risk Analysis Using Risk Register
projects.description: Conducted a security risk assessment using a risk register to quantify the risk likelihood and business impact and prioritize mitigation efforts based on overall risk scores.
projects.title: Quantitative Risk Analysis Using Risk Register
projects.overview: Performed a risk assessment by evaluating operational vulnerabilities and assigning likelihood and impact scores to prioritize risks.

sections.heading: Scenario
content: You are a cybersecurity analyst on a newly formed security team at a commercial bank. The team is conducting an enterprise risk assessment to evaluate the bank's current operational environment and identify security risks that could impact critical systems, data, and business operations. As part of the assessment, the security team is creating a risk register to systematically document, evaluate, and prioritize identified risks.

Your task is to review a defined set of risks recorded in the risk register. For each risk, you will determine the likelihood of occurrence for each identified risk, assess the potential severity and business impact resulting from each risk, and calculate an overall risk score based on likelihood and impact. The resulting scores are used to compare identified risks, prioritize mitigation efforts, and support data-driven decision-making.

sections.heading: Operational Environment
sections.content: The bank is located in a coastal area with low crime rates. Many people and systems handle the bank's data — 100 on-premise employees and 20 remote employees. The customer base of the bank includes 2,000 individual accounts and 200 commercial accounts. The bank's services are marketed by a professional sports team and ten local businesses in the community. There are strict financial regulations that require the bank to secure their data and funds, like having enough cash available each day to meet Federal Reserve requirements.

risks.description: An employee is tricked into sharing confidential information
A database server of backed up data is publicly accessible
The bank's safe is left unlocked
Delivery delays due to natural disasters

sections.heading: Analysis
content: The identified risks pose a significant threat to the bank’s operational environment due to poor security controls, weak encryption practices, and insufficient disaster recovery planning. These gaps increase the likelihood of unauthorized access to sensitive customer and banking data, potentially resulting in substantial financial losses, regulatory penalties, and reputational damage.

Given the volume of sensitive data and the complexity of user and system access, the bank must implement robust security measures to ensure the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of critical information assets.

skills: Security Plan Analysis and Control Documentation
projects.description: Analyzed a data leak incident, reviewed NIST SP 800-53: AC-6, and recommended improvements to enforce principle of least privilege and prevent recurrence.
projects.title: Data Privacy Risk Assessment
projects.overview: Conducted a data risk assessment to evaluate information handling processes and safeguard data privacy.

sections.heading: Scenario
content: You work for an educational technology company that develops an automated grading application used by academic institutions. The application handles a wide range of data that it collects from academic institutions, instructors, parents, and students.

Your team was alerted to a data leak of internal business plans on social media. Preliminary findings indicate that the principle of least privilege was not observed by employees at the company during a sales meeting, contributing to the incident.

The objective of this investigation is to evaluate details about the incident, review the controls in place to prevent data leaks, identify ways to improve information privacy practices, and provide a risk-based justification for how the recommended measures will enhance the organization's overall data security posture.

sections.heading: Incident Summary
content: A sales manager granted team members temporary access to a folder containing confidential internal documents related to an unreleased product, including customer analytics and promotional materials. After the meeting, the manager did not revoke access to the internal folder, but warned the team to wait for approval before sharing the promotional materials externally.

During a video call with a business partner, a member of the sales team forgot the manager's prior warning and proceeded to share the materials without obtaining proper approval. While intending to share the promotional content, the sales representative mistakenly shared a link to the internal folder containing confidential documents. The business partner later posted the link on their company's social media page, resulting in the unintended disclosure of sensitive internal information.

sections.heading: Issues: What factors contributed to the information leak?
items: New product files were labeled 'Internal-Only' rather than 'Confidential', resulting in insufficient access restrictions.
Customer analytics, promotional materials, and internal strategic documents were not properly separated according to sensitivity level, increasing risk exposure.
Access to internal folders was not revoked prior to material sharing, and files were distributed without formal approval.
The manager did not implement stricter data protection measures after identifying policy violations.

Sensitive materials were shared with external parties without adequate verification, and corrective actions (i.e. disabling the link, relocating files to a secure folder) were not taken promptly after the incorrect link was distributed.

sections.heading: Review: What does NIST SP 800-53: AC-6 address?
content: It addresses the Principle of Least Privilege Access, which states that only the least amount of access must be provided to perform the users’ responsibilities.

Referring to the data leak incident, the Least Privilege Access wasn’t implemented as the Sales Manager mistakenly provided access to confidential new product information that hasn’t been publicly released, along with the customer analytics and promotional materials.

Moreover, the manager did not revoke access to his team after the meeting concluded, which led to the accidental sharing of information by the sales team member to their business partner, resulting to more data leakage.

sections.heading: Recommendations: How might the principle of least privilege be improved at the company?
items: Implement data privacy protection controls to ensure that all data is safe and secure
Have a dedicated Security Team to regularly audit permissions and monitor access to company assets
Provide monthly or quarterly employee training on best security practices and how to implement them in real-world scenarios
Provide guidance on how to properly classify assets (Public, Internal-Only, Confidential, Restricted)
Have a single company asset repository for Internal-Only assets and use a separate repository for Public assets or for client-sharing (i.e. SharePoint, Secure shared network drive).

sections.heading: Justification: How might these improvements address the issues?
content: Implementing data privacy protection controls, regular employee training programs that address common security threats, best security practices and how to implement them in real-world scenarios, and proper asset classification, combined with a dedicated Security Team to regularly audit permissions and monitor company asset access will help address the company’s data leakage issues.

These recommendations, along with NIST SP 800-53: AC-6 – Principle of Least Privilege Access, will improve the company’s security posture and help ensure that their assets and customers’ information are securely handled.

projects.projectName: Access Control Analysis Using the AAA Framework
skills: Application of AAA (Authentication, Authorization, Accounting) Framework
projects.description: Reviewed and analyzed security event logs to improve authentication, authorization, and accounting controls for a small business.
projects.title: Access Control Analysis Using AAA Framework
projects.overview: Assessed access control and operational process for a small business, identified security gaps, and provided recommendations to strengthen overall security practices

sections.heading: Scenario
content: You’re the first cybersecurity professional hired by a growing business.

Recently, a deposit was made from the business to an unknown bank account. The finance manager confirmed it was not an error, and fortunately, they were able to stop the payment. The owner has asked you to investigate what happened to prevent any future incidents.

To do this, you will review the access logs, document findings to identify a potential threat actor, analyze exploited access control weaknesses, and recommend mitigations to strengthen controls and reduce the likelihood of recurrence.

sections.heading: Findings
items: All employees were granted administrator-level access within the system.
Log records indicate that contractor Robert Taylor Jr. retained administrative access after his contract ended on December 27, 2019.
The unauthorized transaction occurred on October 3, 2023 at 8:29:57 AM.
Event logs show that the system was accessed from the device 'Up2-NoGud' with IP address 152.207.255.255.

sections.heading: Analysis
items: Granting administrative access to all employees significantly increases the risk of unauthorized transactions, data disclosure, and system misuse.
Absence of granular access control reduces traceability, weakens non-repudiation, and impairs effective incident response.
Inability to enforce role-based access control (RBAC) further exposes vulnerabilities and weak policies.
Failure to restrict system access to authorized, active employees poses internal risks.
Ineffective deprovisioning procedures permit post-termination access, increasing the risk of account abuse and unauthorized activity.

sections.heading: Recommendations
items: Implement strong authentication controls to verify all users, systems, bank accounts, and data accessing company assets.
Conduct regular security awareness training to employee accountability and vigilance, and adherence to security policies.
Leverage incident response teams to efficiently detect, investigate, and resolve security incidents and threats.
Establish and enforce strict policies, standards, and procedures, including immediate revocation of employee access upon termination, to strengthen the company's security posture.

projects.description: Conducted a NIST SP 800-30 vulnerability assessment, evaluating potential risks and creating a report that outlines business impact and remediation strategies.
projects.title: Vulnerability Assessment Report
projects.overview: Performed a vulnerability assessment for a small business to evaluate the security of critical systems, identify potential risks, and provide actionable recommendations to strengthen the organization’s overall security posture.

sections.heading: Scenario
sections.content: You are a newly hired cybersecurity analyst for an e-commerce company that relies on a remote database server to support globally distributed employees. The database, which employees regularly query for customer data, has been publicly accessible since the company’s launch three years ago, creating a significant security vulnerability. You are responsible for conducting a vulnerability assessment and preparing a report outlining the business risks and recommending measures to secure the server.

sections.heading: System Environment
sections.content: The server hardware consists of a powerful CPU processor and 128GB of memory. It runs on the latest version of Linux operating system and hosts a MySQL database management system. It is configured with a stable network connection using IPv4 addresses and interacts with other servers on the network. Security measures include SSL/TLS encrypted connections.

sections.heading: Scope
sections.content: This vulnerability assessment focused on evaluating the effectiveness of current access controls for the publicly accessible database server. The review covered a three-month period from June to August 2025 and assessed risks associated with unauthorized access, data exposure, and potential misuse of organizational information assets.

sections.heading: Methodology
sections.content: The risk analysis was conducted using the framework outlined in NIST SP 800-30 Rev. 1. Threat sources, threat events, and associated vulnerabilities were identified based on the server’s public exposure. Likelihood and severity ratings were assigned using a qualitative three-level scale (Low, Medium, High) in accordance with NIST guidance, and overall risk scores were calculated accordingly.

threatEvent: Disgruntled employees may misuse publicly available business assets

sections.heading: Business Impact Analysis
content: The publicly accessible database presents a critical security weakness. The absence of appropriate access restrictions significantly increases the probability of unauthorized access, data exfiltration, and service disruption.

Exposure of sensitive organizational and customer data could result in financial loss, legal liability, and reputational harm. Given the organization's reliance on remote access and centralized data storage, it is essential to implement stronger access controls, encryption mechanisms, and monitor capabilities to preserve confidentiality, integrity, and availability.

sections.heading: Assessment Limitations
items: Risk ratings were limited to a three-tier qualitative scale.
The scenario provided limited visibility into the company's overall security posture and existing security controls.
The specific types and classification of data stored in the database were not provided, which may affect the accuracy of the impact assessment.

content: Restrict public access to the database by implementing firewall rules or network access control list (NACL) that only allow authorized connections.
Require employees to connect through a virtual private network (VPN) when accessing internal systems remotely to ensure encrypted communication.
Implement role-based access control (RBAC) and enforce the principle of least privilege to limit user permissions to only what's required for their roles.
Enable encryption mechanisms to protect sensitive data both in transit and at rest.
Implement centralized logging and monitoring to detect unauthorized access and malicious activity.
Conduct regular vulnerability assessments and security reviews to identify any misconfigurations or exposed services.
Establish database access logs and monitoring procedures.
Implement routine system updates and patch management to ensure operating system and database software remain secure.
Develop and enforce organizational policies governing database access and data protection.
Provide security awareness training to employees on proper handling of sensitive data.
Establish an incident response process to ensure timely investigation and remediation of security events.

sections.heading: Scenario
sections.content: You are part of the security team at Rhetorical Hospital and discover an unattended USB drive in the parking lot. Recognizing the security risk, you analyze the device using virtualization software in an isolated environment to safely investigate its contents without exposing the hospital’s systems or network to potential malware.

sections.heading: Findings
content: A virtual machine was created to safely analyze the USB drive in an isolated environment. The contents of the device appear to belong to Jorge Bailey, the Human Resource Manager at Rhetorical Hospital.

The device contains a mix of personal and work-related files which include family and pet photos, a new hire letter, an employee shift schedule, employee budget information, and a resume, indicating a lack of separation between personal and corporate data.

sections.heading: Device Analysis
sections.content: The presence of sensitive work files, including employee budget data, a new hire letter, employee scheduling, and a resume, poses a significant security risk. These files contain personally identifiable information (PII) that could be exploited for identity theft, financial fraud, or unauthorized access to corporate resources, potentially resulting to reputational and financial damage to both the employee and the organization.

sections.heading: Attacker Mindset
content: If a threat actor gains access to the device, sensitive information could be exploited for identity theft, financial fraud, blackmail, or unauthorized access to internal systems.

Personal files, such as family photos and event-related documents (‘Wedding list.gslides’), could enable targeted phishing and social engineering attacks against Jorge Bailey and his associates by providing information that increases the likelihood of successful exploitation.
The 'Vacation Ideas' file can provide access to the business, and the content can be misused or sold to its competitors for financial gain.
Such attacks can be used to target Jorge Bailey, the hospital employees, and the entire organization if not mitigated.

sections.heading: Threat Analysis
content: Untrusted, unsecure media devices may contain malware or be embedded with phishing links to bait users in providing sensitive information.
If accessed by an employee, the infected device could compromise their personal data, as well as the company's assets.

The presence of both personal and work-related files increases the risks for malicious attacks such as identity theft, blackmail, ransomware, and social engineering attacks that may lead to financial losses, reputational damage, and loss of trust.

sections.heading: Threat Mitigation
sections.content: To mitigate the identified potential attacks, the company could protect itself and its employees by impelementing a combination of technical, managerial, and operational controls:

content: encrypting data at rest and in transit
creating strict policies and standards to protect the company and its assets
providing security awareness training to employees regularly
leveraging security teams for incident response
